skill.name: firebase-firestore-standard
skill.description: Guides teams through Firestore Standard Edition setup, security rules design, index planning, and modular Web SDK usage.
skill.summary: Build and secure Firestore Standard Edition projects with practical Firebase guidance.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, credential, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples for Firebase CLI usage, Firestore rules syntax, URLs, regex, and attack-check guidance. No prompt injection attempt, obfuscated code, credential exfiltration, or executable package scripts were found. The main residual risk is operational: users may run Firebase CLI commands that change their own project configuration.

low_findings.title: Firebase CLI Commands Can Modify Project State
low_findings.description: The static command findings are mostly false positives because they are Markdown examples, not executable skill code. Some examples use Firebase CLI deploy commands that can modify rules and indexes if a user chooses to run them, so users should review the target project before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain explicit Firebase CLI commands, including deploy examples. They are documentation snippets rather than hidden execution paths, which limits impact to user-approved command use.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Are Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings are benign documentation examples, including a Firebase Console link placeholder, localhost emulator UI, and URL validation regex. No external request code or data exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose, local emulator guidance, or a regex inside Firestore rules examples. I found no code path that sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Sensitive-Term and Weak-Crypto Findings Are Security Guidance
low_findings.description: The certificate, private key, reconnaissance, and weak-crypto static findings occur in security guidance, rule examples, or attack test checklists. They describe threats to prevent rather than implement malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text instructs users to avoid PII exposure, privilege escalation, and insecure rules. No evidence shows credential collection, cryptographic misuse, or system reconnaissance code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Use of npx With Latest Firebase Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends commands that download and run the latest Firebase CLI through npx. This is a common Firebase workflow, but it depends on npm package integrity and user trust in the toolchain.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command pattern is clearly present and can execute downloaded tooling. The context is legitimate Firebase CLI documentation, so this is an operational warning rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Firestore projects need correct setup, rules, indexes, and SDK patterns before launch. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for secure Firestore Standard Edition work.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create Firebase configuration files for Firestore Standard Edition.
Provides secure starting rules that deny access by default.
Guides generation of Firestore Security Rules with validation and authorization checks.
Shows common modular Web SDK patterns for reads, writes, queries, and listeners.
Explains index types, composite index needs, and deployment commands.
Encourages rule validation and adversarial review before launch.
limitations: Covers the modular Web SDK, not every Firebase client or Admin SDK.
Does not replace a full security review for production rules.
Firebase CLI commands require user authentication and project access.
Examples must be adapted to each application data model.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New Firestore Project
use_cases.description: Create the basic Firebase files, locked-down rules, and index configuration needed for a new project.

use_cases.title: Harden Existing Security Rules
use_cases.description: Review collections, user ownership, role fields, and validation gaps before publishing an app.

use_cases.title: Plan Queries and Indexes
use_cases.description: Map application queries to Firestore index requirements and deployment steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Firestore Starter Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firestore Standard Edition for my Firebase project. Include the files I need and safe initial rules.

prompt_templates.title: Add Web SDK Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use the modular Firestore Web SDK for creating, updating, reading, querying, and listening to documents.

prompt_templates.title: Design Rules From My Data Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Firestore collections and generate prototype security rules with ownership checks, validation functions, and clear assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Review Rules Against Attack Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a Firestore rules reviewer. Test my rules for privilege escalation, PII exposure, schema pollution, invalid updates, and query mismatches.

output_examples.input: I need a safe starting point for Firestore rules.
output_examples.output: A locked-down rules plan that denies access by default, lists assumed collections, and explains where owner checks and validators will be added.

output_examples.input: My app queries cities by state and population.
output_examples.output: A query review that identifies the likely composite index need and explains how to deploy the index configuration.

output_examples.input: Review my user profile rules before launch.
output_examples.output: A security review that checks PII separation, immutable fields, role changes, update validation, and attacker test cases.

best_practices: Start with deny-by-default rules and add only required access paths.
Use validator functions for both create and update operations.
Validate rules with emulator tests or dry runs before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not allow broad authenticated reads for documents that contain private user data.
Do not rely only on ownership checks without schema and field validation.
Do not deploy generated rules without reviewing project IDs, data models, and access assumptions.

faq.question: Does this skill run Firebase commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents Firebase CLI commands that a user may choose to run in their own environment.

faq.question: Can it create production-ready security rules?
faq.answer: It can create strong prototypes, but production rules still need review, testing, and project-specific validation.

faq.question: Which Firestore SDK does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on the modular Web SDK and common browser application patterns.

faq.question: Does it explain Firestore indexes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains single-field indexes, composite indexes, exemptions, and deployment commands.

faq.question: Is it useful for existing Firebase projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help review current rules, indexes, queries, and setup files.

faq.question: What should I verify before deploying?
faq.answer: Verify the target project, rule syntax, data model assumptions, index changes, and authorization behavior.
